model_output_raw_text: {"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 64","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Use more examples within the fixed 256-step budget by increasing batch size, paired with a modest LR increase to keep optimization progress strong under the larger batch.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The current configuration is still a small MLP for a fixed-step full-dataset regime. A modest width increase should improve representational capacity without adding much complexity or likely exceeding the runtime budget.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"DEPTH = 3","old":"DEPTH = 2","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The wider two-layer MLP is still improving cleanly, so adding one more hidden layer is a direct next capacity increase. This keeps the design simple while giving the flat classifier more nonlinear depth within the same fixed-step budget.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 20","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The deeper MLP responded well, which suggests it is still capacity-limited. A moderate additional width increase is the simplest follow-up and should strengthen the representation without changing the overall training recipe.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 0.0","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"This run is still in a short-horizon, likely underfit regime with clean labels. Removing weight decay is a simple way to reduce optimization drag on the now-larger MLP and may convert more of the fixed 256-step budget into fit quality.","secondary_modes":["indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.2e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 384","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The current recipe is strong but still budget-limited at a fixed number of optimizer steps. Increasing batch size should expose the model to substantially more training examples within those 256 steps, and a small LR bump keeps update magnitude aligned with the larger batch.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 512","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 384","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The strongest gain so far came from showing the model more examples within the fixed 256-step budget. Pushing batch size further is the cleanest continuation of that trend while preserving the rest of the successful recipe.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 1024","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 512","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The last two improvements came from increasing batch size under the fixed 256-step budget, which effectively gives the model more training examples without changing the core recipe. Pushing to 1024 is the cleanest continuation of that trend.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 2048","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 1024","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Increasing batch size has been the dominant source of improvement in this fixed-step regime because it lets the model process more total examples while keeping the successful optimizer and schedule intact. Moving from 1024 to 2048 is the simplest next continuation of that trend.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.8e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.2e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Batch size has scaled up dramatically and is now the main driver of progress, so the next low-complexity step is to raise the learning rate to better match the lower-gradient-noise regime. This keeps runtime fixed while testing whether the current recipe is under-stepping at batch 2048.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.2e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.8e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The higher learning rate regressed validation loss at batch 2048, so the simplest and most evidence-backed next step is to revert to the previous better scale before exploring anything else.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"CHANNEL_MULT = 3","old":"CHANNEL_MULT = 2","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The run now uses a very large effective data budget from batch 2048, so a modest capacity increase is a reasonable next lever. Raising the channel multiplier enlarges the deeper hidden layers without changing the training recipe or first-layer input bottleneck.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 24","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 20","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The larger-batch recipe is still responding to capacity increases, and with `CHANNEL_MULT = 3` a moderate bump to the base width will strengthen the first hidden layer and push the deeper layers closer to the existing cap without changing the training dynamics.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"DEPTH = 4","old":"DEPTH = 3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The model is still improving from controlled capacity increases under the large-batch regime. Adding one more hidden layer is the simplest next way to increase expressiveness now that width and deeper-layer scaling are already paying off.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 28","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 24","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"With `CHANNEL_MULT = 3` and `DEPTH = 4`, the later hidden layers are already at the cap, so the cleanest remaining capacity lever is to widen the first hidden layer. Increasing the base width should improve the initial projection while preserving the rest of the successful recipe.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.2e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The latest capacity increase plateaued while a higher learning rate previously hurt, so the most plausible remaining scalar adjustment is a modest LR reduction to trade a bit of step aggressiveness for better refinement in the current deeper, wider model.","secondary_modes":["layout","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 8e-4","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"A modest LR reduction just improved the deeper, wider large-batch model, so the final low-complexity follow-up is to continue in the same direction and test whether a bit more refinement helps within the fixed 256-step budget.","secondary_modes":["layout","micro"]}
